body:
## Goal
Cleanly finish the follow-up after adopting Backstage as the self-hosted IDP target: align the issue tracker, repo structure, and docs so contributors no longer pick up stale Port-runtime tasks.

## Context
- Backstage has been selected as the target path in ADR 0003.
- GitHub remains the versioned source of truth.
- `port/` remains legacy migration reference unless a later explicit decision removes it.
- Existing open issues still include old Port-specific work and newer Backstage/self-hosted work.

## Scope
- Update issues #17-#24 to name Backstage explicitly and point to current repo artifacts.
- Supersede/close stale Port runtime issues #8-#14 where appropriate.
- Reframe #15 as the Backstage validation/demo dry-run gate.
- Update `backstage/migration-issues.md` with the cleaned issue mapping.
- Run a stale-language pass over landing docs and navigation.
- Keep the MVP boundary: no production infrastructure changes, Kubernetes automation, or autonomous compliance enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The active issue queue uses Backstage-specific titles and acceptance criteria.
- [ ] Old Port workspace/import/view/action issues are closed or clearly superseded with replacement links.
- [ ] `backstage/migration-issues.md` matches the tracker state.
- [ ] Landing docs and navigation point to Backstage as the active target and Port only as migration history.
- [ ] Validation evidence is recorded for YAML and manual consistency checks.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Changes are committed on a branch linked to this issue.
- [ ] The plan file is included in the branch for traceability.
- [ ] The branch is pushed for review.

## Plan
See `agents/archive/hermes/plans/2026-05-21_075416-clean-backstage-follow-up.md` in the implementation branch.
title: Backstage IDP MVP: Clean follow-up after target switch

body:
## Goal
Make the self-hosted IDP MVP easy to run for local review and demo purposes.

## Scope
- Define the local runtime command.
- Add environment variable documentation.
- Provide seed/demo data loading instructions.
- Document the demo script for stakeholders.
- Optionally add a container setup if it stays lightweight and does not imply production Kubernetes automation.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A new contributor can run the portal locally from a clean checkout using documented commands.
- [ ] Demo data loads without manual copy/paste.
- [ ] The demo path covers catalog overview, service detail, scorecards, and GitHub issue workflow or documented mock/fallback.
- [ ] Any required secrets are listed in an example env file or docs without real values.
- [ ] The deployment story stays MVP-only and avoids production infrastructure promises.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Local demo instructions are tested.
- [ ] README and demo docs are updated.
- [ ] Remaining non-MVP deployment work is explicitly deferred.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Package and document local/demo deployment

body:
## Goal
Update repository documentation and validation so the project clearly targets a self-hosted IDP MVP instead of a Port workspace MVP.

## Files likely affected
- `README.md`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `wiki/docs/vision.md`
- `wiki/docs/demo-story.md`
- `wiki/docs/operating-model.md`
- `wiki/docs/iks-metadata-model.md`
- `.github/workflows/validate-port-config.yml` or its replacement
- `github/workflows/validate-port-config.yml` or its replacement

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] README describes the self-hosted portal approach and local run path.
- [ ] Existing Port references are either removed, marked as legacy migration inputs, or moved to a compatibility section.
- [ ] Demo story reflects the self-hosted UI instead of Port screens.
- [ ] Validation workflow name and paths match the new catalog/app structure.
- [ ] Agent guidance no longer instructs contributors to create/configure a Port workspace for the MVP.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Docs consistently describe one MVP direction.
- [ ] GitHub Actions validation still passes.
- [ ] Any meaningful direction change is backed by an ADR.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Update repository structure, docs, and validation away from Port runtime assumptions

body:
## Goal
Replace Port self-service actions with self-hosted portal workflows that create auditable GitHub issues.

## Current inputs
- `port/actions/create-github-issue-for-missing-metadata.yaml`
- `port/actions/onboard-service-checklist.yaml`
- `port/actions/request-iks-review.yaml`
- `github/issue-templates/catalog-metadata-fix.md`
- `github/issue-templates/iks-review.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Portal offers an action to create a metadata-fix issue for a service.
- [ ] Portal offers an action to request an IKS review for a service.
- [ ] Created issues include service identifier, missing field/reason, and relevant catalog links.
- [ ] Workflow is auditable and low-risk: it creates GitHub issues only.
- [ ] Credentials/secrets needed for GitHub issue creation are documented and not committed.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Action flow is tested against demo services.
- [ ] Generated issue content matches the existing issue-template intent.
- [ ] No production system mutation is introduced.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Replace Port self-service actions with GitHub issue workflows

body:
## Goal
Replace Port scorecards with self-hosted scorecard/completeness checks for catalog quality and IKS metadata.

## Current inputs
- `port/scorecards/catalog-quality.yaml`
- `port/scorecards/iks-baseline.yaml`

## Required checks
- Technical owner assigned.
- System assigned.
- Repository linked.
- Lifecycle classified.
- Documentation link present.
- Runbook link present.
- Business owner assigned.
- Criticality classified.
- Protection need classified.
- Data class classified.
- IKS scope declared through `complianceScope` containing `iks`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Scorecard logic runs against the self-hosted catalog data.
- [ ] Results are visible in the portal UI or generated as a local/CI report.
- [ ] `customer-portal` passes the intended baseline.
- [ ] `reporting-api` fails the runbook check intentionally.
- [ ] Checks are advisory only and do not block teams in the MVP.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Scorecard behavior is tested or validated with sample data.
- [ ] Documentation explains how to interpret scorecard results.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Recreate catalog quality and IKS scorecards

body:
## Goal
Implement the catalog views that replace the Port service catalog demo screens.

## Required views
- All services.
- IKS-relevant services where `complianceScope` contains `iks`.
- Critical services where `criticality` is `high` or `critical`.
- Service detail page showing owner, business owner, system, repository, lifecycle, documentation, runbook, criticality, protection need, data class, and compliance scope.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `customer-portal` is visible as the complete demo service.
- [ ] `reporting-api` is visible with its intentionally missing runbook.
- [ ] Filters support the demo story previously planned for Port.
- [ ] Relations between services, systems, repositories, and teams are visible or linked.
- [ ] UI clearly distinguishes MVP demo data from production data.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Demo flow can be performed in the self-hosted portal UI.
- [ ] README or demo docs describe the navigation path.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Implement service catalog views

body:
## Goal
Create the minimal self-hosted portal application/runtime needed to render the MVP catalog locally and in a self-hosted environment.

## Scope
- Scaffold the selected portal stack from the architecture decision.
- Add local development instructions.
- Add a minimal home/catalog page.
- Read catalog data from the repository source-of-truth format.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A developer can start the portal locally with documented commands.
- [ ] The portal shows at least a catalog overview page.
- [ ] The portal reads versioned catalog data from the repository, not hardcoded UI fixtures.
- [ ] The runtime does not require Kubernetes for the MVP.
- [ ] Basic CI validation/build steps are added or documented.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] New application files are committed in a clearly named directory.
- [ ] README explains how to run the portal locally.
- [ ] Validation/build command succeeds in CI or is documented with a clear fallback.

## Out of scope
- Production deployment.
- Authentication/authorization hardening beyond a documented MVP placeholder.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Scaffold portal application/runtime

body:
## Goal
Define the catalog data model for the self-hosted IDP MVP while preserving the useful metadata already present in the Port-oriented YAML files.

## Current inputs
- `port/blueprints/*.yaml`
- `port/entities/*.yaml`
- `examples/services/*/catalog.yaml`
- `wiki/docs/iks-metadata-model.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A self-hosted catalog schema is documented for service, system, team, repository, and agent/workflow context if still needed.
- [ ] Required MVP service fields are preserved: owner, businessOwner, system, repository, lifecycle, criticality, protectionNeed, dataClass, documentation, and complianceScope.
- [ ] The intentionally incomplete `reporting-api` runbook case remains representable for demo purposes.
- [ ] A migration mapping from Port blueprints/entities to the new schema is documented.
- [ ] Validation expectations are defined for the new schema.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Documentation is updated in `wiki/docs/` and/or repository docs.
- [ ] Example catalog files exist in the selected self-hosted format.
- [ ] No production infrastructure or Kubernetes-specific model is introduced.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Define catalog schema and GitHub source-of-truth format

body:
## Goal
Replace the Port-based MVP direction with a self-hosted Internal Developer Portal MVP and capture the architectural decision.

## Context
The repository currently models the MVP around Port blueprints, entities, scorecards, actions, and automations. The new direction is to keep GitHub as source of truth but render and operate the portal through a self-hosted solution.

## Scope
- Compare viable self-hosted options, for example Backstage, a lightweight custom app, or another open-source portal stack.
- Choose the MVP target architecture.
- Define what existing `port/` artifacts become: migration input, deprecated reference, or transformed catalog model.
- Keep MVP boundaries intact: no Kubernetes automation, no production infrastructure changes, no autonomous compliance enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A decision record is created under `wiki/decisions/` explaining why Port is replaced.
- [ ] The ADR identifies the selected self-hosted portal approach.
- [ ] The ADR documents major tradeoffs: operations burden, extensibility, auth, catalog model, workflows, and demo effort.
- [ ] Follow-up implementation issues are linked from the ADR or issue comments.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Wiki submodule change is committed and pushed.
- [ ] Main repository records the updated wiki submodule pointer.
- [ ] Existing README/project docs no longer imply Port is the required runtime once the ADR is merged.

## Out of scope
- Implementing the portal UI in this issue.
- Migrating all YAML artifacts in this issue.
title: Self-hosted IDP MVP: Decide target architecture and record ADR

body:
## Goal
Validate the repository artifacts and dry-run the MVP demo end-to-end before presenting it.

## Validation targets
- `port/`
- `examples/`
- `agents/`
- `wiki/docs/demo-story.md`
- `wiki/docs/iks-metadata-model.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] YAML validation runs locally or in GitHub Actions.
- [ ] Manual consistency pass confirms required service fields are documented, modeled, populated, and scorecarded where relevant.
- [ ] Demo follows `wiki/docs/demo-story.md` from catalog overview through GitHub issue action.
- [ ] Known limitations are documented.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Demo can be completed without changing production systems.
- [ ] Remaining risks are documented for stakeholders.
- [ ] MVP scope still excludes Kubernetes, runtime health, provisioning, and autonomous compliance enforcement.
title: MVP: Run validation and complete demo dry-run

body:
## Goal
Decide whether the draft metadata drift automation is part of the MVP demo or remains documented future work.

## File
- `port/automations/notify-metadata-drift.yaml`

## Context
The automation is currently marked `status: draft`. The MVP boundary avoids autonomous compliance enforcement.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Decision is made: keep as draft, remove from demo path, or configure as advisory-only issue notification.
- [ ] If included, it only creates auditable follow-up and does not enforce compliance.
- [ ] If deferred, the demo script clearly treats it as future path.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] File and docs are consistent with the decision.
- [ ] Any meaningful governance choice is captured in `wiki/decisions/` if needed.
title: MVP: Decide whether metadata drift automation stays draft or enters demo

body:
## Goal
Connect the MVP Port actions to GitHub issue creation so catalog gaps and IKS review requests become auditable work items.

## Files
- `port/actions/create-github-issue-for-missing-metadata.yaml`
- `port/actions/onboard-service-checklist.yaml`
- `port/actions/request-iks-review.yaml`
- `github/issue-templates/catalog-metadata-fix.md`
- `github/issue-templates/iks-review.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A Port user can create a metadata-fix GitHub issue from a service.
- [ ] A Port user can request an IKS review as a GitHub issue from a service.
- [ ] Placeholder notes in action definitions are replaced or documented as intentional demo limitations.
- [ ] Issue body includes enough service context for manual follow-up.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Action is tested against a demo service.
- [ ] Created issue can be traced back to the originating service.
- [ ] Workflow remains advisory and auditable.

## Out of scope
- No production system changes.
- No automatic approvals or compliance decisions.
title: MVP: Wire low-risk GitHub issue self-service actions

body:
## Goal
Create the Port workspace that will host the MVP service catalog demo.

## Context
The MVP uses Port as the portal, catalog, scorecard, and workflow surface. GitHub remains the source of truth.

Relevant docs:
- `README.md`
- `wiki/docs/vision.md`
- `wiki/docs/operating-model.md`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Port workspace exists and is accessible to the demo stakeholders.
- [ ] MVP boundary is confirmed: no Kubernetes automation, no runtime health dashboard, no infrastructure provisioning.
- [ ] Required access for importing blueprints and entities is available.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Workspace URL and responsible owner are documented in the appropriate project notes or handover channel.
- [ ] Required Port/GitHub integration prerequisites are known.

## Out of scope
- No autonomous compliance enforcement.
- No production system mutation.
title: MVP: Create Port workspace for the IDP catalog demo

body:
## Task

Evaluate and add SonarCloud integration for this repository if feasible.

## Context

- This repository is a docs-and-configuration MVP, not an application runtime.
- The repo currently emphasizes Port configuration, demo entities, docs, examples, and agent assets.
- Existing workflow templates live under `github/workflows/`; active GitHub Actions workflows would need to live under `.github/workflows/`.
- No existing SonarCloud configuration was found in the repository.

## Proposed scope

Add a low-risk SonarCloud setup that fits the repo's current shape:

- Decide whether SonarCloud is useful for this docs/config repo, given the limited source-code surface.
- If useful, add the minimum required configuration, likely including:
  - `sonar-project.properties`
  - an active GitHub Actions workflow under `.github/workflows/` if automatic scanning is desired
  - documentation of required GitHub/SonarCloud secrets or organization setup
- Avoid adding runtime, Kubernetes, deployment, or infrastructure automation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Feasibility is documented: what SonarCloud can meaningfully scan in this repo and what it cannot.
- [ ] Required SonarCloud organization/project setup and secrets are listed.
- [ ] If implemented, SonarCloud configuration is minimal and scoped to repository quality checks.
- [ ] If an active workflow is added under `.github/workflows/`, the change summary explicitly calls out that this activates real GitHub Actions behavior.
- [ ] Existing `github/` template files are not moved or repurposed unless that is explicitly decided.
- [ ] Local validation covers any changed YAML/properties files.

## Notes / open questions

- Which SonarCloud organization should own the project?
- Should quality gate enforcement be required for PRs, or should the first iteration only publish analysis results?
- Is this repository's limited code surface enough to justify SonarCloud, or should repo hygiene stay with YAML/Markdown validation for now?
title: Evaluate and add SonarCloud integration
